title: Help Desk Hours and Support Requests

export_view.value:
Our Help Desk is designed to streamline your support experience, making it easy to submit and manage support tickets.

  • Submit Support Requests: Quickly submit support tickets through our application or Support website.

  • Direct Communication: Stay connected with our global Support Team to discuss your concerns and ask questions.

  • Track Progress: Monitor the status of your support tickets and receive timely updates on any issues.

To ensure your support request is handled quickly and securely, please use the email address associated with your User Profile in RepairQ when creating a Help Desk user account and when filling out the “Customer Email Address” field when submitting a request. This address is used for verifying your identity and determining your available permissions for the request. Following this process helps us address your concerns securely and efficiently, helping our users receive the best support experience.

Note: Please update your Approved Senders List with the following email addresses to ensure you receive our response: no-reply@automation.atlassian.com, jira@automation.atlassian.com, and jira@servicecentral.atlassian.net. If you have not received a response promptly, we also recommend checking your spam and trash folders before sending a follow-up to our team.

Additional Resources

  • Create a Help Desk User Account

  • RepairQ Help Desk - Knowledge Base and Submit a Request

  • RepairQ Help Desk - Viewing Requests and Status Updates

  • RepairQ Help Desk - Organization Management

  • RepairQ Support Login Authorization

Help Desk Hours of Operation

RepairQ Support monitors tickets during regular business hours: 9a to 5p CST/CDT, Monday through Friday. When we receive a request, we will work with you to resolve the issue promptly.

RepairQ Support Login Authorization

RepairQ includes added security measures, allowing in-app authorization for RepairQ Support team members to access your database. These allow the Support Team to log into your database to investigate issues and assist with support tickets and even impersonate users to research those issues from the requester's point of view. To grant our Support Team access to your environment, click here for instructions on granting Support access.

Contact our Help Desk via Support Request

If you experience an issue with RepairQ or other ServiceCentral software solutions and need to contact our Support Team, click here to submit a Support Request. Please send your support request from the email address associated with your User Profile in RepairQ. This helps us quickly verify your identity and provide the best possible assistance. We encourage you to save the link in your Bookmarks for future use.

Verify Your Email Address (RepairQ Users)

  • Log in to your RepairQ Location and navigate to your Staff Profile.

  • Confirm the email address listed in your profile matches the one you use for support requests.

Support Request Details

When submitting a request, include the following details to help our team identify the root cause and resolve the issue more quickly:

  • RepairQ URL - Please provide your database URL, located in the address bar of your browser. For example, businessname.repairq.io or 401b-ca1001.repairq.io.

  • Troubleshooting Details - Provide details about the task leading up to the issue.

    • What you were doing - Describe the task or workflow you were performing before the issue occurred.

    • Steps to reproduce - List the actions you took that led to the issue, so we can recreate it in our testing environment.

    • Observed behaviors - Explain what you experienced, such as error messages, unexpected behavior, or performance issues.

  • Screenshots/Recordings - Attach screenshots or screen recordings that illustrate the issue. Visual aids can help our Support Team better understand your issue.

    • Windows Snipping Tool - Open the tool by searching for "Snipping Tool" in the Start menu or use the shortcut (Windows Key + Shift + S) to open the Snip & Sketch tool for capturing part of the screen.

    • macOS Shortcuts - Use shortcuts to capture screenshots of your entire screen, a selected area, or a specific window, and also provide options for screen recording.

      • Shift + Command (⌘) + 3 - Captures the entire screen.

      • Shift + Command (⌘) + 4 - Captures a selected portion of the screen.

      • Shift + Command (⌘) + 5 - Opens the screenshot toolbar for more options, including capturing specific windows.

Report a Critical issue

If you encounter a critical issue that prevents you from accessing RepairQ or performing essential business functions—such as situations where normal operations are completely halted—please contact our Help Desk at supportteam@servicecentral.com. Be sure to include the word "Emergency" in the subject line of your request and provide us with as much supporting information as possible regarding your issue. Our team will review your request and respond as quickly as possible. The Support Team may provide a workaround for your request until our Development Team can resolve the issue.

START 1.22.X
